name: Button

variants.name: primary
variants.description: Main call-to-action. Use for the single most important action on a page or in a dialog.
variants.when_to_use: One primary button per view. Confirms, submits, or proceeds.
variants.avoid: Do not use more than one primary button in the same context.

variants.name: secondary
variants.description: Supporting action alongside a primary button.
variants.when_to_use: Cancel, back, or secondary confirm actions.
variants.avoid: Do not use as the sole action — pair with primary or as standalone tertiary.

variants.name: destructive
variants.description: Irreversible or dangerous action such as delete or remove.
variants.when_to_use: Delete, remove, revoke actions. Always pair with a confirmation dialog.
variants.avoid: Do not use for navigation or reversible actions.

variants.name: tertiary
variants.description: Low-emphasis action, no border or background at rest.
variants.when_to_use: Inline actions, contextual links within a layout.
variants.avoid: Do not use as the primary action in a form or dialog.

icon_only.note: When true, label is used as aria-label only

loading.note: Replaces label with spinner, keeps button width stable

label.note: Fixed size — does not adapt in responsive Small mode

icon_gap.note: Gap between icon and label text

usage_rules.detail: Never render more than one variant=primary button in the same form, dialog or section.
A destructive button must always be paired with an Alert Dialog or confirmation step before executing the action.

usage_rules.rule: Always use Text.textAction for button labels
usage_rules.detail: Do not use any other typography token for button labels. textAction is fixed at 14px SemiBold regardless of responsive mode.

usage_rules.rule: Use semantic tokens only — never raw palette values
usage_rules.detail: Never use primitive tokens (e.g. dsBlue/500) directly. Always use Action.*, Text.*, Border.* semantic tokens.

usage_rules.rule: Pair border color with correct border width token
usage_rules.detail: Border.Focused must always be paired with Border.Width.Focus (2px). Border.Standard must use Border.Width.Standard (1px). Do not mix states.

usage_rules.rule: Disabled buttons must not trigger actions
usage_rules.detail: When disabled=true, the button must be non-interactive. Do not rely on opacity alone.
When icon_only=true, the label prop is used as aria-label and must be descriptive.
If a button is placed inside a container, the button radius (Border.Radius.Standard 12px) must be smaller than the container radius (Border.Radius.Large 16px).

accessibility.aria_disabled: Use aria-disabled=true rather than HTML disabled when button should remain focusable

confidence_blockers: If ticket does not specify variant, ask for clarification — do not assume primary.
If ticket requests multiple primary buttons, flag as usage_rule violation before generating.
If ticket requests a destructive action without a confirmation dialog, flag and suggest Alert Dialog pairing.
agent_instructions.token_resolution: Always resolve colors from design-tokens.json using the correct theme (light/dark). Never hardcode hex values in generated code.
agent_instructions.responsive_note: Text.textAction does not change in Small mode. Spacing.Component tokens do not change. Only Spacing.Layout tokens vary — these are not used inside the Button component itself.

embedding_text: Button component. Variants: primary CTA action, secondary supporting action, destructive delete remove action, tertiary low-emphasis inline action. States: standard hovered pressed disabled focused. Uses Action color tokens, Text.textAction typography, Spacing.Component padding, Border.Radius.Standard 12px, focus ring Border.Focused 2px. One primary per view. Destructive requires confirmation dialog.
